description: Smoke test run to verify pi-autoresearch init/run/log pipeline in agent-config

asi.hypothesis: pi-autoresearch extension tools are installed and can initialize, run a command, parse METRIC output, and record a result end-to-end
asi.rollback_reason: Smoke test only; not a real optimization attempt and should not be kept as a project change
asi.next_action_hint: Use /skill:autoresearch-create with a real benchmark target next
asi.learned: Manual install works even though npm global install failed under Nix; core experiment pipeline is functioning

description: Establish baseline for session-recap latency with 7-sample median harness

asi.hypothesis: Measure the common session-recap path before code changes using a repeated-run median to reduce noise
asi.rollback_reason: Baseline only; no optimization change yet
asi.next_action_hint: Apply a low-risk allocation reduction in extract_messages and re-measure
asi.learned: Current baseline is ~38.2ms median with ~4.0ms spread on agent-config/pi session recap path

description: Use a bounded deque in extract_messages so session-recap keeps only the final N messages instead of building and slicing the full list

asi.hypothesis: For long session files, retaining only the final N messages with deque(maxlen=N) should reduce allocation and copying overhead without changing output semantics
asi.learned: Median latency improved from 38.179ms to 35.095ms on the common agent-config/pi recap path; variability increased on this sample so the win should be rechecked in a later run
asi.next_action_hint: Consider a confirmation run or inspect directory scan cost in find_session_files if more speed is needed

description: Avoid per-line strip before json.loads and lazily allocate text/tool lists during content extraction

asi.hypothesis: Removing an extra line copy and skipping empty list allocation for common content shapes might reduce recap parsing overhead
asi.rollback_reason: This change regressed the primary metric versus the current best kept version (35.095ms → 36.387ms) and increased variability
asi.next_action_hint: Revert this path and investigate session directory scanning or repeated timestamp formatting instead
asi.learned: The extra parsing/allocation tweaks were not better than the simpler deque-only improvement on this workload

description: Switch find_session_files directory walks to os.scandir to test whether DirEntry-based scanning reduces session discovery overhead

asi.hypothesis: Directory traversal may dominate the common recap path, and os.scandir could lower filesystem iteration overhead versus Path.iterdir
asi.rollback_reason: This regressed the primary metric versus the current best kept version (35.095ms → 37.487ms) with no compensating benefit
asi.next_action_hint: Revert and investigate output formatting costs such as repeated timestamp conversion, or run a confirmation measurement on the kept deque optimization
asi.learned: On this workload, DirEntry-based scanning did not beat the existing Path-based traversal

description: Add a dedicated HH:MM:SS timestamp formatter for message rows to avoid formatting full datetimes then splitting on 'T'

asi.hypothesis: Message-row timestamp formatting may be a small but repeated hot path, so formatting only HH:MM:SS directly could trim per-message string work
asi.rollback_reason: This did not improve the primary metric versus the current best kept version (35.095ms → 37.489ms), despite slightly lower spread
asi.next_action_hint: Revert and prefer a confirmation run of the kept deque optimization or a more structural hypothesis backed by profiling
asi.learned: Micro-optimizing the timestamp formatting path was not worthwhile on this benchmark

description: Re-run the current kept deque implementation without code changes to confirm the earlier improvement against benchmark noise

asi.hypothesis: If the deque-based optimization is robust, a no-change confirmation run should stay near the previously observed 35.095ms result
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation run was slower than the current best kept result (35.095ms → 40.092ms), so there is no new improvement to keep
asi.next_action_hint: Treat this workload as noisier than the first keep suggested and either strengthen the benchmark harness or profile before further micro-optimizations
asi.learned: The same code measured materially slower on this confirmation run, indicating substantial benchmark noise or environmental variance on this path

description: Bound session discovery to the needed newest files using a small heap instead of always collecting and fully sorting all matching session JSONLs

asi.hypothesis: The common recap path only needs skip+sessions newest files, so keeping only a bounded top-K set during discovery may reduce unnecessary sorting work
asi.rollback_reason: This did not beat the current best kept implementation (35.095ms → 38.043ms) and was only roughly baseline-level
asi.next_action_hint: Revert this optimization attempt and consider improving benchmark stability before pursuing more micro-optimizations
asi.learned: Session file sorting was not the dominant cost for this workload; top-K bounding did not translate into end-to-end speedup
